summaries.168625: Confirmed Android-only on latest stable (3.29.3) and master (3.32.0-pre), verified on S10+ running Android 11. Two PRs attempted fixes: #169715 and #173511; one was closed and the other may be eligible for reopening.
summaries.96572: Confirmed on macOS and Linux; native macOS shows the accent menu instead of repeating. Native Linux and Windows behavior was not determined in comments. Issue stalled pending clarification of platform-native behavior.
summaries.158584: Confirmed on macOS and Android in addition to Linux (originally reported). The _BorderContainer CustomPaint shrinks from 72px to 56px when switching from UnderlineInputBorder to OutlineInputBorder with a label present. Routed to @victorsanni for confirmation on whether the size mismatch is intentional.
summaries.169264: Root cause identified: Flutter intentionally sets a different VisualDensity for desktop/web vs mobile via ThemeData, causing the padding difference. PR #166834 introduced InputDecoration.visualDensity and InputDecorationTheme.visualDensity to address this. Related to #166201.
summaries.181907: Single triage acknowledgment comment only; no root cause, platform confirmation beyond Windows, proposed fix, or status update provided.
summaries.156184: Confirmed still reproducible on iOS 18 (not fixed unlike sibling issue #156183). Reproduced on stable 3.24.3 and master 3.26.0-pre. iOS-only (iPad simulator with Portuguese Portugal keyboard).
summaries.164544: Confirmed iPad-specific; works on iPhone with same app. A commenter suggested the issue may be that keyboard events interact with the framework textfield rather than UITextInput directly, and a potential fix would be to have UITextInput handle the key input.
summaries.72364: Issue repurposed to explore using SecSharedCredentials API on iOS for saving credentials to the correct associated domain. Assignee (@LongCatIsLooong) was auto-unassigned after prolonged inactivity with no status updates.
summaries.159670: Root cause identified in a later comment: after first keystroke, the autofill popup dismissal triggers PHASE_CLIENT_ON_CONTROLS_CHANGED which cancels the IME and destroys TextInputConnection. Gboard is not affected; only third-party IMEs (Sogou, WeChat) exhibit this. Confirmed on Android through stable 3.41.2 with Pixel 10/Android 16. Assignee auto-removed after inactivity.
summaries.159580: Confirmed more prominent on web and desktop platforms. Text selection also overlaps the scrollbar. A workaround (wrapping TextField in Scrollbar) avoids the overlap but is not considered valid. Still reproducible in 3.38.2.
summaries.170512: Confirmed web-specific; works on iOS and macOS. Reproduced on stable 3.32.2 and master 3.33.0-pre. Redirected to web triage team.
summaries.94965: Comments discuss scope: ESC should deselect selected text, unfocus focused text fields, and cancel tab-based keyboard navigation. Related issues #98163 and #84416 cited. Discussion about matching platform-native behavior. WCAG 2.1 compliance raised as an accessibility concern. No PR or fix landed.
summaries.105244: Root cause identified: Firefox sends a null composing region in the same message as the final committed text, while Chrome sends two separate updates. This causes composing text (e.g., 'ni') to not be replaced by the composed symbol until the next keypress. Related to engine PR flutter/engine#33590 but confirmed not fixed by it.
summaries.179526: Root cause identified: _SelectableTextContainerDelegate._calculateLocalRange does not account for discontinuous selection when non-selectable WidgetSpans break the selectable range, especially when selecting backwards. Issue only affects backward selection; forward selection produces reasonable ranges. No PR linked.
summaries.60825: Comments confirm the issue is about the macOS application-level Edit menu. Expected behavior: enable/disable menu items based on Flutter text/selection state asynchronously. Paste action via the menu should propagate to the focused TextField. No fix landed.
summaries.78762: Confirmed reproducible on API 26 (not API 27 as originally stated). Similar bugs noted on Mac (#78629) and iOS (#79844) but with different root causes. Question of current reproducibility on API 26 raised without definitive answer.
summaries.141775: Android is also affected: SelectionArea context menu only shows Copy and Select All, while SelectableText shows Share, Read aloud, Translate, etc. A workaround using AdaptiveTextSelectionToolbar.editable with custom callbacks is provided for iOS. A commenter noted Android additional buttons may now be present on latest master.
summaries.172526: Issue redirected as a package-level limitation but then reopened as a proposal. Root cause is that WidgetSpan is treated as a single character internally, making it impossible to delete chip-by-chip via backspace. Reference made to Android's ImageSpan API. #56285 noted as related. No fix or PR proposed.
summaries.99211: Confirmed Samsung-only feature (Samsung One UI); not present on Pixel or Xiaomi devices. Tagged e:samsung. Moved to framework for evaluation of implementing drag-to-move selection controls.
summaries.46737: A workaround using TextEditingController and FocusNode listener is provided in comments. The team lean toward not implementing the property natively given the workaround is straightforward, though original reporter says they wouldn't mind if it stayed low-priority.
summaries.122433: Discussion identifies platform-specific spell checker APIs: NSSpellChecker (macOS), ISpellChecker (Windows), and libenchant (Linux). A super_editor plugin for macOS spell/grammar check exists but is unreleased pending Flutter's own implementation. No PR landed for desktop spell check.
summaries.129583: Discussion notes opportunities to reuse logic from EditableText. Several gesture PRs landed. Remaining unimplemented gestures include iOS-specific cursor drag, long press to move cursor, and some Android/Linux behaviors. Agreed to watch for code-sharing opportunities.
summaries.104950: Confirmed reproducible on macOS stable and master. The issue is that pressing backspace on a composing character (e.g., opt+u diacritic) requires two presses to delete it. Also noted to affect regular Flutter TextField, not just super_editor.
summaries.91861: Comments note the issue may also affect Windows with a video demonstrating the Windows IME backspace issue. Issue may be a duplicate of #92050 for Windows. Stalled with no fix landed.
summaries.118267: Community requests to extend InputDecorationTheme with a baseStyle property to set TextField input text style globally without affecting other widgets. A Korean font issue (crossbox characters for incomplete syllables) is mentioned as further motivation. No fix or PR landed.
summaries.130030: A PR #130039 was linked that attempts to parameterize the hard-coded 4.0 floatingLabelGap value. Comments note the current formula includes an unexplained 4.0 constant. Workaround: use InputDecorator.label with a Transform widget to move the floating label.
summaries.154565: Confirmed Android-specific; works as expected on iOS. Root cause tentatively attributed to difference in how iOS TextField loses focus (allowing keyboard dismiss) vs Android. No fix landed.
summaries.160606: Root cause identified: the autocorrect selection highlight is drawn by iOS natively, not Flutter, so CupertinoThemeData primaryColor has no effect. misspelledTextStyle and kMisspelledSelectionColor are constants not derived from the theme. No iOS API found to change this color.
summaries.106418: Issue stems from a RangeError in _CharacterBoundary when startIndex is invalid during delete action on Android 12. Only one production occurrence reported via Sentry. Team kept open for further reports but requires a reproducible sample.
summaries.99164: Confirmed pressing ENTER to select an autocomplete item loses focus on the text field. Related to #98199. No PR landed. Labeled as feature request.
summaries.117771: A PR #118283 was proposed to change the notification flow from TextInput to TextInputConnection to TextInputClient. Separate discussion about IME candidate window positioning stuck in upper-left corner on Windows/macOS when using custom TextInputClient.
summaries.169269: Root cause not definitively found. Specific to add-to-app with remotely hosted Flutter module via CocoaPods; works locally. Working on 3.22 but broken from 3.24. Issue cannot be reproduced by the team.
summaries.162311: Confirmed Android with Gboard on OnePlus-style device (not reproducible on S10+). Reporter clarified this is separate from TLHC issues and is specific to Hybrid Composition when the widget expands downward. Team unable to reproduce.
summaries.166400: Confirmed still present in stable 3.29.2. Issue is Windows 10-specific; works fine on Windows 11. Team has only Windows 11 available and cannot verify directly.
summaries.34610: Early comment identified root cause: ParagraphStyle directionality is LTR for mixed text, causing spaces to be inserted at the wrong end of Arabic spans. The SkParagraph switch (#39420) was expected to fix many behaviors. Subsequent comments report partial improvements but deletion with opposite-direction keyboards remains broken.
summaries.77957: Single comment links the issue to its originating PR #77730.
summaries.182876: Root cause identified: (1) Flutter's AXPlatformNodeWin does not properly expose IValueProvider/ITextProvider patterns for UIA; (2) Flutter uses legacy IMM32 instead of TSF, blocking TSF-based tools like Win+H dictation. A comment suggests Flutter turns on the accessibility tree lazily, preventing dictation tools from interacting with text fields.
summaries.107732: OverlayPortal PR #105335 noted as potentially making this fix easier. A developer also requested exposing a selectionOverlayTransitionBuilder to allow custom toolbar fade animations, noting the current private implementation makes customization very difficult.
summaries.171319: Confirmed Windows-specific with Chinese IME; macOS is fine. Issue appears device-specific (notebook computers) and not reproducible by the team.
summaries.91738: Discussion notes the Bidi class exists in Flutter's intl. Suggestion to add an auto value similar to HTML's dir attribute. Noted as technically feasible via ICU ubidi API. Adding to TextPainter suggested, along with needing a low-level API for WidgetSpan writing directions.
summaries.134902: Single comment suggests registering a font variation axis in the axis registry before adding to Icon.
summaries.79497: Single doctor output comment confirming reproduction environment; no root cause or fix discussion.
summaries.35064: Discussion clarifies this may be a breaking change since subclasses override maxLines as int; suggestion to use num type instead. Assignee (@gaganyadav80) was auto-removed after inactivity. Team member expressed openness to a PR.
summaries.151292: Root cause identified: the customer's index.html was missing a DOCTYPE declaration, causing Chrome Quirks mode. In Quirks mode, form elements have an unexpected margin-block-end: 1em CSS causing the flt-text-editing-host to grow by 16px per form added. Fix needed: reset margin CSS for form elements and warn developers about missing doctype.
summaries.61344: A workaround is proposed (displaying error in a separate Text widget) but reporter notes it is hacky. No formal fix or PR landed.
summaries.154692: Confirmed on Android with Microsoft SwiftKey AI Keyboard; reproduces with English-to-Japanese translation when there is a trailing space after the source word. Reproduced on stable 3.24.2 and master 3.25.0-pre on Pixel 7 Android 14.
summaries.184012: Reproduced on physical Pixel Fold running Android 16 with TalkBack 16.2.0, but not on emulators or older TalkBack versions. Reverting TalkBack to 16.0.0 fixes the issue. Identified as likely a TalkBack 16.2.0 regression in how it interprets Flutter's accessibility tree.
summaries.168857: Root cause identified: when system context menu's Select All is pressed, the update comes through updateEditingValue classified as keyboard cause, which prevents selection handles from being shown. Proposed fix: use a custom Select All button calling editableTextState.selectAll(SelectionChangedCause.toolbar).
summaries.150813: The autocorrect highlight is drawn by iOS, not Flutter, so fixing is non-trivial. PR #150770 was ruled out as the culprit. Team asked whether the offset is proportional to font size, and suggested moving FlutterTextInputView as a last resort workaround. Related to #150815.
summaries.31985: Workarounds provided: SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide') after build (causes visible keyboard animation). The didChangeViewFocus API is now available (noted in a recent comment), potentially enabling a proper fix.
summaries.63170: Discussion confirms autofillHints already accepts multiple strings, so 'shipping' could be passed as part of the list. Challenge is applying the section correctly as a prefix to the autocomplete attribute. Mobile use case for sections is unclear.
summaries.19779: Comments note this is not a breaking change since the sync API would remain. Changing autocorrect to depend on keyboardType breaks many tests. Suggestion to default autocorrect to false for email fields.
summaries.167512: Root cause suspected: Flutter's iOS keyboard brightness is not updated dynamically while the keyboard is visible, unlike native iOS which updates immediately. Potential fix: hide/reshow keyboard or reset text input connection when didChangePlatformBrightness fires. Connection to @LongCatIsLooong's UITextView prototype noted.
summaries.168743: Root cause identified: tap on selection causes platform to dismiss system context menu (Flutter responds by hiding toolbar), then Flutter also responds to the tap and tries to toggle the toolbar back on. Proposed fix: track timestamp of most recent onDismiss callback and skip present if within a threshold (~0.1s).
summaries.123415: Comments confirm the user-visible impact is essentially 'triple-tap does not work' especially on short words or single characters. Reproducible by triple-clicking a single letter on iOS. Two proposed solutions involve using IgnorePointer or a pass-through widget during double-tap to allow the third tap to reach TextSelectionGestureDetector.
summaries.41324: Confirmed reproducible with a code sample; video demonstration added. A commenter noted they implemented a solution fixing RTL alignment inconsistencies in InputDecoration.
summaries.118536: Flutter run key f to dump focus tree was added in PR #123473. A DevTools issue (#5326) tracks adding focus tooling in DevTools. debugDumpFocusTree and debugFocusChanges boolean were noted as existing tools.
summaries.55343: Discussion clarifies that onTap of TextField cannot notify the IME (runs in a different process). Suggestion to add TextInput.onclicked to TextInputChannel. Routed to framework/design team. No PR or fix.
summaries.107613: Confirmed Chrome OS-specific. Additional reproduction code provided using Card widget with multiple TextFormFields. Works differently on Chrome OS vs Windows tablet mode. No root cause or fix provided.
summaries.50834: Single comment attempting to reproduce the affinity issue could not replicate it on latest master, questioning if it is still valid.
summaries.103446: The didChangeViewFocus API is now available (noted in a recent comment), potentially unblocking a fix. A contributor expressed interest in implementing the fix for macOS. macOS still shows incorrect non-greyed selection when window loses focus.
summaries.183808: Discussion clarifies the goal is to give Flutter Android developers the ability to set strings announced before the role announcement, matching what native Android and iOS can do. As-is, both label and hint are announced after role on Android, while label is announced before on iOS.
summaries.122647: Discussion includes a DartPad demo showing M3 TextField customized to look like CupertinoTextField. Spellcheck API on iOS is noted as broken with TextField (word suggestions overflow). Team members express interest in synchronizing TextField and CupertinoTextField APIs or building a Material-based adaptive TextField.
summaries.182803: Root cause identified: MacWhisper uses CGEvent keyboard simulation (goes through NSResponder chain), while Flutter's FlutterTextInputPlugin only handles NSTextInputClient protocol messages. A commenter linked #178609 as potentially causal. A community workaround using AXUIElement accessibility API insertion is described. Moved to team-macos.
summaries.9688: Long-running feature request with workarounds described: (1) use local state and call validate() when async result returns; (2) use flutter_form_bloc package. Proposal to add AsyncForm/AsyncTextFormField/AsyncFormState without breaking existing API. A DartPad example is provided. No PR landed.
summaries.34897: A commenter attempted to reproduce with nested MaterialApp but could not. The issue may require a specific combination of Container/Card widgets. No root cause or fix.
summaries.176283: Confirmed on iOS 18.1.1 stable 3.35.4 and master 3.37.0-pre. Related to an old issue comment at #13771. The leading space from iOS dictation is noted as a long-standing issue.
summaries.181532: Confirmed on both latest stable and master, and also reproducible on desktop (not just web). No root cause analysis or fix proposed.
summaries.120836: Discussion notes the Android caret height TODO may still exist. A hack is identified for zero-width/height placeholder bounds in getGlyphInfo returning RTL direction and zero-size rect.
summaries.133614: Only triage acknowledgment and label comments; no root cause or fix discussed. Issue remains open for Linux team tracking.
summaries.159674: Confirmed Safari-specific; does not occur in Chrome. Reproduced on stable 3.24.5 and master 3.27.0-pre.
summaries.118725: Root cause discussed: native UIKit apps create a UITextField, set isSecureEntry=false, and grab its layer to hide content from screenshots/recordings. Flutter needs to implement similar layer-based hiding. Keyboard hiding while obscured already works, but password content hiding and dynamic switching do not.
summaries.82999: IME fullscreen landscape mode is an Android-specific feature. A community plugin (orient_text_field on pub.dev) was created as a workaround. Flutter team categorized this as potentially better suited for a package.
summaries.149557: Discussion confirms the goal is validation on focus-exit only. The autovalidateMode does not cover this case. Request is for an onFocusExit callback on TextField/TextFormField. No fix landed.
summaries.19702: Workaround provided: use TextInputFormatter to detect key differences between oldValue and newValue. No formal fix or PR.
summaries.92506: Workaround provided for text-containing WidgetSpans: use SelectionArea instead of SelectableText.rich to enable copying. No solution for image WidgetSpans or rich clipboard content.
summaries.54104: Workarounds provided: set errorStyle to TextStyle(height: 0.001) to hide error text while preserving error border styling; however these workarounds create layout side effects. No formal API to suppress error text display while still validating.
summaries.78572: Confirmed on Windows 10 master. Windows jumps to start of next word in both directions, while Mac jumps to end when moving right and start when moving left. No fix or PR.
summaries.100041: Confirmed: when FlutterViewController is created via initWithEngine, _engineNeedsLaunch is NO so addInternalPlugins is not called, leaving keyboardManager nil and breaking external keyboard input. Soft keyboard works but physical/Bluetooth keyboard does not. Team requested a minimal reproducible sample.
summaries.157859: Root cause: even with TextInputType.none, the platform TextInputControl still receives updates. Using readOnly=true loses cursor blinking. Custom keyboard scrolling also broken because scroll-to-field relies on View.of(context). Issue escalated to full proposal for filtering which TextInputControls can access a connection.
summaries.184616: Confirmed: macOS shows context menu and allows paste with canRequestFocus:false, same as Windows. Team agrees on normalizing to web behavior (scroll only, no context menu, no selection handles). Fix deferred until after decoupling work in #184093 is complete.
summaries.143999: Root cause identified: CMD+V goes through Flutter's keyboard handler which programmatically accesses the pasteboard (triggering paste warning), while native apps use private iOS APIs unlocked by default handlers. Removing custom handlers allows default paste without warning but may break KeyboardListener widget. Labeled P3 as iPad hardware keyboard-only.
summaries.174689: Root cause not identified. Confirmed on Android, iOS, and desktop when maxLines or minLines is set; does not occur on web. Native Android and iOS apps do not highlight trailing whitespace. Routed to @LongCatIsLooong.
